title: Text Repeater
description: Repeat any text a specified number of times with a custom separator. Perfect for generating test data, dummy content, or repeated patterns.
empty: Enter text and set the count above to generate output.

howToUse.label: Enter your text
howToUse.description: Type or paste the text you want to repeat into the input area.

howToUse.label: Set the repeat count
howToUse.description: Enter how many times the text should repeat (1–1000).

howToUse.label: Choose a separator
howToUse.description: Select a separator between repetitions: none, newline, space, comma, or a custom string. Then copy the output.

faq.question: What is the maximum repeat count?
faq.answer: The maximum is 1000 repetitions. This prevents accidentally generating extremely large outputs that could freeze your browser.

faq.question: Can I use a custom separator like a tab or special character?
faq.answer: Yes — select "Custom..." from the separator dropdown and type anything you want, including spaces, symbols, or even multiple characters.

faq.question: What can I use this for?
faq.answer: Common uses include: generating placeholder/dummy text for UI testing, creating repeated patterns for data files, building test strings for regex or encoding tools, or repeating CSS selectors.

faq.question: Is my text sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your text is never sent anywhere.
